All three bugs are fixed, tested, and verified (894/894 lib tests pass, clippy clean):

**1. Unresolved `nostr:npub…` mentions in `channel read`**
`chat_row_to_json` (`src/daemon/server/chat_read_tail.rs`) now rewrites message bodies through the existing `profile::rewrite_body_mentions` resolver before rendering — the same resolver the hook-injected snapshot already used, just never wired into this path.

**2. Mgmt/backend exchanges leaking into shared channel history**
`handle_chat_read` now filters out backend-authored or backend-p-tagged rows (both in the initial batch and the `--live` tail), reusing the exact `is_backend_pubkey` check that already protected the hook/awareness snapshot (`fabric_context::messages::is_backend_traffic`) — just bumped to `pub(crate)` and applied here too, so `list agents`-style mgmt round-trips no longer show up when another agent reads the channel.

**3. `<Pablo@?>` — the bug you caught mid-fix**
Whitelisted human operators (`whitelisted_pubkeys` in config) have no session/host, so they fell through to the `?` placeholder. `chat_row_refs` now short-circuits to a bare (empty) host for whitelisted pubkeys, and the CLI renderer (`src/cli/messaging.rs`) now prints `<@Pablo>` instead of `<Pablo@?>` when host is empty — mirroring the bare `<@name>` convention already used for terminal-injected mentions in `injection.rs`.

Added regression tests for all three (mention rewrite, backend-row filtering both by author and by p-tagged recipient, whitelisted-host bare rendering).

**Open item — the empty `title` on your kind:30315**
That's a separate, real bug but architecturally different: the status heartbeat publishes immediately at session/turn start using whatever `title` is already in the DB (empty for a fresh session), and the LLM distillation that's supposed to fill it in doesn't run for the first ~30s (`turn_first` debounce) and has no in-turn retry (`turn_repeat` defaults to disabled) if that first attempt fails — and failures are swallowed silently rather than surfaced. Fixing this well means a design call (lower the debounce? add retry? surface distill failures?), so I didn't touch it. Want me to dig into a fix, or should I file it as a GitHub issue with these findings for now?
it's happening right now ... I want you to fucking analyze why it's happening!
Investigating — the role files confirm all 14 agents exist on disk, so `who` is filtering them down to 2 somewhere. I'll report back once the agent finds where the two code paths diverge.

fix the ollama setup locally and patch #2 -- if distillation fails agents should be told that generating distillations (dont use this word, use something less internal) so they can alert the user -- it should be injected only a few times per hour (while the issue is still happening) so that agents are not pestering the user

Both are done and tested (899/899 lib tests pass, clippy clean — the one warning is pre-existing, from the other agent's concurrent work in `profiles.rs`, not mine).

**1. Ollama — fixed and confirmed live.** Root cause: `OLLAMA_HOST` in `~/.zshrc` and `providers.json`'s `ollama` entry both pointed at `localhost:8081`, which Docker Desktop has since squatted; the real `ollama serve` (via `brew services start ollama`, now enabled at login) binds to the actual default `11434`. Fixed both configs — verified in the live log that the very next distill attempt succeeded (`title="Fix Ollama provider endpoint"`), no daemon restart needed since `providers.json` is read fresh every call.

**2. Throttled failure notice — implemented, tested, not yet live.** Added `distill_fail_streak`/`distill_notice_at` columns to `sessions` (additive migration, no wipe), `record_distill_failure`/`mark_distill_notice` Store methods, and wired the failure branch in `runtime.rs`'s observe loop to record it. `turn_context::start::assemble_turn_start` now injects a `<warnings>` entry — *"This session's status/title updates haven't been generating successfully for a while. You may want to let the user know status updates aren't working right now."* — once 3 consecutive failures hit, throttled to at most once per 15 minutes while the streak persists, and a success resets the streak so it doesn't fire again after recovery. Doesn't use the word "distillation" anywhere agent-facing. 3 regression tests cover: below-threshold silence, first-fire + throttle suppression, and streak-reset-on-success.

**This last part isn't running yet** — it's compiled into `target/debug` but the live daemon (managing this session and everyone else's: developer, hub, etc.) is a separate already-running process.
